text: Fill in the blanks about the pectoral region and breast anatomy.
questions: Pectoralis major has three heads of origin: the *clavicular* head, the *sternocostal* head, and the abdominal head.
All three heads of pectoralis major converge to insert on the lateral lip of the *bicipital groove/intertubercular sulcus* of the humerus.
Pectoralis minor lies deep to pectoralis major and inserts on the *coracoid* process of the scapula.
The clavicular head of pectoralis major is supplied by the *lateral pectoral* nerve, while the sternocostal head receives supply from both the lateral and *medial pectoral* nerves.
The most common route of lymphatic drainage of the breast is via the *axillary* lymph nodes, which receive about *75:percent* percent of the drainage.
